title: Apogeul inadecvării – 'premierul' Nicușor Dan la Consiliul pentru Pace
summary: The article harshly criticizes Romanian Prime Minister Nicușor Dan's participation at the inaugural Peace Council meeting organized by Trump in Washington, arguing his poor English, diplomatic gaffes, and submissive attitude exposed his inadequacy for leadership. The author contends that Romania's alignment with Trump over European partners is a strategic error, as Trump does not represent enduring American values and European allies are deliberately distancing themselves from his initiatives.
talking_points: Nicușor Dan's poor English skills, uncontrolled laughter, and protocol failures at the Peace Council demonstrated his unsuitability for high office
Romania failed to coordinate with European partners before attending the event, isolating itself from EU allies who deliberately avoided association with Trump's Peace Board

Equating the United States with Trump is a major analytical error — Trump does not represent American values and his presidency will be studied as a case of institutional vulnerability and power pathology
Nicușor Dan's use of Orthodox religious language ('har Domnului') was a calculated electoral signal representing 'belonging without believing' characteristic of Romanian religiosity

Those who voted against Georgescu and Simion to get a pro-European, non-sovereignist president were deceived — Nicușor Dan is a masked sovereignist who neither practices nor understands European values
author: Andrei Colțea
